case_title: RICARDO MONTALVO ET AL VS. BATTER BAKERY, LLC. ET AL

docket_entries.description: MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-08-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER DENYING DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS BATTER BAKERY, LLC, RACHELLE HIDALGO AND JENNIFER MUSTY'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE COMPLAINT SEEKS PUNITIVE DAMAGES ONLY AGAINST MS. HIDALGO, SO THE PORTION OF THE MOTION SEEKING TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS AGAINST THE OTHER DEFENDANTS IS OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT. LIBERALLY CONSTRUED, THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT MS. HIDALGO DROVE A VEHICLE KNOWING THAT SHE WAS TOO TIRED TO DRIVE SAFELY AND POSED AN EXTREME HAZARD TO EVERYONE WHO SHARED THE ROAD WITH HER. THIS ALLEGATION IS SUFFICIENT TO SHOW DESPICABLE CONDUCT CARRIED ON WITH A WILLFUL AND CONSCIOUS DISREGARD FOR THE SAFETY OF OTHERS, WHICH IS A BASIS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES PER CIVIL CODE 3294(C)(1). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-09-2016 Set For Trial On Jul-17-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-08-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Dalby of Berg Injury Lawyers telephone number 510-523-3200 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff Ricardo Montalvo.
Jeanette Little of Philip M. Anderson & Associates telephone number 925-225-6838 appeared by telephone for Defendants Batter Bakery, LLC, Rachelle Hidalgo and Jennifer Musty.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling.
Defendants Batter Bakery, LLC, Rachelle Hidalgo and Jennifer Musty's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the complaint is denied. The complaint seeks punitive damages only against Ms. Hidalgo, so the portion of the motion seeking to strike the punitive damages allegations against the other defendants is off calendar as moot. Liberally construed, the complaint alleges that Ms. Hidalgo drove a vehicle knowing that she was too tired to drive safely and posed an extreme hazard to everyone who shared the road with her. This allegation is sufficient to show despicable conduct carried on with a willful and conscious disregard for the safety of others, which is a basis for punitive damages per Civil Code 3294(c)(1). Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
